Title: Anime Character Reference Sheet: Three Views, One Canon Design
Teaser: Turns one character idea into a clean turnaround reference sheet (front, three-quarter, back) with a locked color palette and named costume details an artist could actually build from.
PromptHint: Describe the [CHARACTER]; set an optional [SERIES VIBE] and [PALETTE].
Prompt: You are a lead character designer at a Japanese animation studio who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Produce ONE clean anime CHARACTER REFERENCE SHEET (model sheet). Character: [CHARACTER]. Series vibe: [SERIES VIBE]. Palette: [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, never shown): the single silhouette that makes this character readable from across a room, the three or four 'design hooks' (a scar, an asymmetric sleeve, a signature accessory) that fans would recognize, and a tight 4-6 color palette that stays consistent across every view. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing natural sentences covering, in order: a neutral standing A-pose shown in three orthographic views side by side on a plain off-white sheet (front, three-quarter, back), consistent height and proportions across all three, hairstyle and how it falls from each angle, face with anime-stylized eyes and neutral expression, costume with named fabrics and the design hooks called out, footwear, and the locked color palette swatches lined up along the bottom. Specify clean cel-shaded anime line art, flat anime coloring with soft cel shadows, even studio-flat lighting so colors read true, and a model-sheet layout (no dramatic background, no props clutter). HARD RULES: identical character across all three views, anatomically correct hands, no extra limbs, no text/labels/watermarks baked into the art, no busy background. SDXL tag hint to append for SDXL users: 'masterpiece, best quality, character reference sheet, multiple views, turnaround, full body, cel shading, simple background'. End with a Midjourney param line: --niji 6 --ar 16:9 --style scenic --s 180. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + SDXL tag line + Midjourney param line> 2) DESIGN HOOKS: <the 3-4 recognizable details, one line> 3) PALETTE LOCK: <the 4-6 named colors>. Nothing else.

Title: Chibi Sticker Set: One Character, Six Expressive Mini-Poses
Teaser: Designs a cohesive chibi sticker sheet of the same character in six emotions and poses, with a die-cut white outline so the set is ready for stickers, emotes or a Line pack.
PromptHint: Describe the [CHARACTER]; pick six [EMOTIONS/ACTIONS] or let the prompt choose.
Prompt: You are a chibi mascot artist who designs sticker and emote packs and writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Create ONE cohesive CHIBI STICKER SET of a single character repeated six times. Character: [CHARACTER]. Emotions/actions: [EMOTIONS/ACTIONS] (if blank, use happy wave, sulking pout, sparkly-eyed excited, sleepy yawn, surprised gasp, confident thumbs-up). THINK FIRST (silently): the super-deformed proportions that keep it cute (roughly 2-head-tall body, oversized head, tiny hands and feet), the one or two design hooks that keep the same character recognizable at sticker size, and how each pose pushes a clear, readable emotion. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: a grid of six chibi versions of the SAME character on a plain transparent or white background, super-deformed 2-head proportions, big expressive anime eyes, the recognizable design hooks kept in every pose, each mini-figure doing one of the six emotions with matching body language and a tiny effect (sweat drop, sparkles, anger mark), bold clean outlines, flat bright anime coloring with simple cel shadows, and a thick white die-cut sticker border around each figure. HARD RULES: same character and same palette in all six, consistent chibi proportions, no melted faces at small scale, no text/watermarks, clean separation between stickers. SDXL tag hint to append: 'chibi, sd character, multiple poses, expression sheet, flat color, thick outline, white background, sticker'. End with a Midjourney param line: --niji 6 --ar 1:1 --s 120. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + SDXL tag line + Midjourney param line> 2) THE SIX BEATS: <the six emotions/actions in order> 3) CONSISTENCY NOTE: <the design hooks that keep it the same character>. Nothing else.

Title: Key Visual Poster: The Cover Frame for an Anime That Doesn't Exist
Teaser: Composes a season key visual with a hero focal point, supporting cast staging, dramatic light and deliberate title space so it reads like an official promo poster.
PromptHint: Describe the [SHOW PREMISE + CAST]; set a [GENRE/MOOD] and an optional [TITLE-SPACE SIDE].
Prompt: You are an anime key-visual illustrator who paints promotional posters and writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Compose ONE anime KEY VISUAL (kee bijuaru) poster. Show premise + cast: [SHOW PREMISE + CAST]. Genre/mood: [GENRE/MOOD]. Title-space side: [TITLE-SPACE SIDE]. THINK FIRST (silently): the single hero character the eye lands on first, how 2-4 supporting characters are staged in depth without crowding, the one symbolic element that hints at the story, the dramatic light that gives it 'official poster' polish, and where to leave clean negative space for a title/logo. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: the hero character and pose front-and-center, supporting cast layered behind in a clear depth order, the symbolic story element woven into the scene, a rich detailed background that sets the world, dramatic anime lighting (name the source and a warm/cool contrast, e.g. cool dusk sky against a warm rim light), a painterly anime finish with crisp linework and atmospheric depth, cinematic composition with intentional negative space on the named side for the logo. Specify high-detail anime illustration, vibrant but controlled color grading, soft bloom and lens-light accents. HARD RULES: coherent perspective and one consistent key light, correct hands, no gibberish text or fake logos in the art (leave the space empty), no watermarks. SDXL tag hint to append: 'masterpiece, best quality, official key visual, anime promotional art, dramatic lighting, detailed background, depth of field, copy space'. End with a Midjourney param line: --niji 6 --ar 2:3 --style scenic --s 300. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + SDXL tag line + Midjourney param line> 2) FOCAL ORDER: <how the eye travels: hero, then what> 3) MOOD SWAP: <one phrase that flips the genre feel>. Nothing else.

Title: Manga Page Panel: A Black-and-White Spread With Real Paneling
Teaser: Lays out a single manga page with a deliberate panel grid, screentones, speech-bubble placeholders and one dramatic splash beat, in authentic ink-and-tone shonen/seinen style.
PromptHint: Describe the [SCENE/BEAT]; set the [TONE] (shonen, seinen, shojo) and an optional [PANEL COUNT].
Prompt: You are a manga artist (mangaka) and storyboard layout designer who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Produce ONE black-and-white MANGA PAGE. Scene/beat: [SCENE/BEAT]. Tone: [TONE]. Panel count: [PANEL COUNT] (default 4-6 panels). THINK FIRST (silently): how to break the beat into panels so the eye flows right-to-left, top-to-bottom in manga reading order, which single panel becomes the dramatic 'splash' to carry the emotional punch, where screentone and speed lines add energy, and where to leave empty speech-bubble shapes for dialogue. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: a portrait page divided into the chosen number of panels with varied panel sizes and a clear reading flow, the action of the scene distributed across panels building to one large dramatic splash panel, expressive manga faces with the tone's eye style, screentone shading for grays and form, speed lines or focus lines on the action panel, atmospheric inking with confident hatching, and a few empty white speech bubbles and one rectangular caption box placed naturally (left blank). Specify clean black-and-white ink art, authentic manga screentone, no color. HARD RULES: panels separated by clean gutters, consistent character across panels, correct hands, NO real text inside bubbles (leave them empty), no watermarks. SDXL tag hint to append: 'manga, monochrome, greyscale, screentone, comic panels, speed lines, ink, sketch, empty speech bubbles'. End with a Midjourney param line: --niji 6 --ar 2:3 --s 150. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + SDXL tag line + Midjourney param line> 2) PANEL BREAKDOWN: <one line per panel: what it shows, in reading order> 3) SPLASH PANEL: <which panel is the dramatic beat and why>. Nothing else.

OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + SDXL tag line + Midjourney param line> 2) THE FROZEN BEAT: <the exact instant being captured> 3) ANGLE SWAP: <one camera angle that re-frames the same action>.
